name: spherical-ml-biodiversity — what spherical ML is, why it matters, and a worked replication on Copernicus data tied to biodiversity
description: Pedagogical and supporting-evidence repository operationalising the spherical-ML claim from dggs-biodiversity-bias. Three pedagogy notebooks demonstrate (01) the failure mode of flat CNNs on global lat-lon rasters, (02) rotation equivariance — what spherical CNNs preserve and flat CNNs don't, with quantitative demos showing 50.6% pixel disagreement when treating rotation as translation and 100% to 55% peak-response collapse of an equator-cap detector across latitudes, and (03) why HEALPix is the right substrate for spherical ML — equal-area cells + iso-latitude rings + NESTED bit-shift hierarchical refinement + graph structure for DeepSphere-style graph CNNs. Forthcoming notebooks 04 to 08 will replicate Defferrard et al. 2020 DeepSphere atmospheric-river detection on ERA5 Copernicus C3S, add marine-heatwave detection on Copernicus Marine SST, tie both to biodiversity-impact literature (riparian Annex I habitats for ARs; coral / kelp / fish for marine heatwaves), and stack everything on the common HEALPix substrate. This is the spherical-ML side of the ESA GRID4EARTH case for Ellipsoidal HEALPix as a Common DGGS bridging Copernicus EO and Destination Earth.

referencePublication.name: HEALPix: A Framework for High-Resolution Discretization and Fast Analysis of Data Distributed on the Sphere
DeepSphere: efficient spherical convolutional neural network with HEALPix sampling for cosmological applications
